task: A requested audit of the Publish pipeline against Governing Rule 5, followed by fixing every real violation found. Confirmed every Publish render call site already routes through the same SlideRenderer.buildPayload()+.render() the editor uses, but found two real, pixel-confirmed divergences. Violation 1 (High): none of the four Publish render call sites (Read screen, Almost Ready cover, Celebration cover, Story Book PDF/Story Carousel PNG) opted into adaptiveViewport:true, so a Scene authored at a non-portrait Aspect Ratio (e.g. Museum Gallery's own real Landscape Showcase Representation) rendered clipped/mispositioned in every Publish output. Fixed the three literal page previews with adaptiveViewport:true plus dynamic wrapper aspect-ratio where needed; fixed the two fixed-format destinations (PDF/Carousel) with a two-step render -- correct adaptive intermediate canvas, then a new _fitCompositeInto contain-fit/letterbox composite into the destination's own unchanged fixed coordinate space. Violation 2 (Medium): a custom-font race where _ensureCanvasFont's redraw-once-loaded callback only ever redrew the live editor canvas, plus a deeper pre-existing bug found investigating it -- the function checked readiness at an implicit weight but always loaded a hardcoded 700, so a normal-weight Text object's check could never be satisfied by editor OR Publish. Fixed by threading the object's real fontWeight through both check and load consistently, and adding a new exported SlideRenderer.preloadFonts(slide) that PublishStudio.open() now fires the instant Publish opens. Verified via three new Playwright tests plus full regression across the existing Creator/Builder suite and both goldenBuild.js suites, zero console errors. No Theme Contract/Engine V2 Scene Model/Builder change; confined to renderer/slideRenderer.js, js/publishStudio.js, and js/storyDestinations.js.
